description: Cross-framework mapping from CSDDD due diligence obligations to ESRS S2 (Workers in the Value Chain)
sourceFrameworkName: EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (2024/1760)
targetFrameworkName: ESRS S2 — Workers in the Value Chain
notes: Mapping based on the interplay between CSDDD due diligence process requirements and ESRS S2 disclosure requirements. CSDDD mandates the due diligence process; ESRS S2 mandates disclosure of policies, actions, targets, and metrics related to value chain workers. Companies subject to both must align their CSDDD due diligence process with ESRS S2 reporting.

mappings.source_name: Integrating due diligence into policies and management systems
mappings.target_name: Policies related to value chain workers
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 7 requires adoption of a due diligence policy; ESRS S2-1 requires disclosure of policies related to value chain workers including due diligence. The CSDDD policy feeds directly into S2-1 disclosure.

mappings.source_name: Integrating due diligence into policies and management systems
mappings.target_name: Processes for engaging with value chain workers about impacts
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 7 requires processes to implement due diligence; ESRS S2-2 specifically requires disclosure of engagement processes with value chain workers. CSDDD policy integration is broader but encompasses stakeholder engagement.

mappings.source_name: Identifying and assessing actual and potential adverse impacts
mappings.target_name: Processes for engaging with value chain workers about impacts
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 8 requires identification of impacts including stakeholder consultation; ESRS S2-2 requires disclosure of how the undertaking engages with value chain workers and their representatives to understand impacts.

mappings.source_name: Identifying and assessing actual and potential adverse impacts
mappings.target_name: Description of processes to identify and assess material impacts, risks and opportunities
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 8 impact identification aligns with the ESRS 2 cross-cutting requirement to describe the process for identifying and assessing material IROs, including for value chain workers.

mappings.source_name: Identifying and assessing actual and potential adverse impacts — prioritisation (Art 9)
mappings.target_name: Processes to remediate negative impacts and channels for value chain workers to raise concerns
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 8/9 prioritisation based on severity aligns with ESRS S2-3's requirement to disclose approaches to remediation and how severity of impacts is determined.

mappings.source_name: Preventing and mitigating potential adverse impacts
mappings.target_name: Taking action on material impacts on value chain workers, and approaches to managing material risks and pursuing material opportunities related to value chain workers, and effectiveness of those actions
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 10-11 prevention and mitigation obligations directly align with ESRS S2-4's requirement to disclose actions taken on material impacts on value chain workers.

mappings.source_name: Bringing actual adverse impacts to an end
mappings.target_name: Taking action on material impacts on value chain workers
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 12 remediation of actual impacts maps to ESRS S2-4 disclosure of actions taken to address actual impacts.

mappings.source_name: Bringing actual adverse impacts to an end — remediation
mappings.target_name: Processes to remediate negative impacts and channels for value chain workers to raise concerns
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 12 remediation requirements (including providing remediation where company caused impact) directly align with ESRS S2-3 remediation disclosure.

mappings.source_name: Establishing and maintaining a complaints procedure
mappings.target_name: Processes to remediate negative impacts and channels for value chain workers to raise concerns
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 14 complaints procedure maps directly to ESRS S2-3's requirement to disclose channels through which value chain workers can raise concerns.

mappings.source_name: Monitoring the effectiveness of due diligence
mappings.target_name: Taking action on material impacts — effectiveness of those actions
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 15 monitoring effectiveness maps to ESRS S2-4's requirement to disclose the effectiveness of actions taken.

mappings.source_name: Monitoring the effectiveness of due diligence
mappings.target_name: Targets related to managing material negative impacts, advancing positive impacts, and managing material risks and opportunities
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 15 monitoring feeds into target-setting and tracking disclosed under ESRS S2-5.

mappings.target_name: Transition plan for climate change mitigation
mappings.notes: CSDDD Art 22 transition plan obligation maps directly to ESRS E1-1 climate transition plan disclosure, though this is an environmental (not S2) disclosure.
